summary: The United Arab Emirates runs a National Post-Quantum Migration Programme under its Cyber Security Council, anchored by a National Encryption Policy whose executive regulation was approved in November 2025 and which requires government entities to hold officially approved plans to move from traditional encryption to post-quantum cryptography. The Council has deployed a national Crypto Discovery Tool with QuantumGate, customised to the requirements of the National Cryptography Center, and in May 2026 signed an agreement with the Advanced Technology Research Council to scale quantum-safe solutions as part of one of the first coordinated national migration plans. The Dubai Electronic Security Center has issued its own post-quantum cryptography guideline for the emirate of Dubai.
processParticipation: NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography (additional digital signatures call) | research contributor; the Technology Innovation Institute Cryptography Research Centre submitted the MiRitH, PERK and RYDE signature schemes
migrationTimeline:
2025 | National Encryption Policy and its executive regulation approved, mandating officially approved transition plans from traditional encryption to post-quantum cryptography
2026 | Cyber Security Council signs agreement with the Advanced Technology Research Council to deploy and scale quantum-safe solutions across critical national infrastructure
govActors:
UAE Cyber Security Council | Issues the National Encryption Policy and runs the National Post-Quantum Migration Programme and the national Crypto Discovery Tool
ATRC | Advanced Technology Research Council, partners on the national post-quantum security transition and the Crypto Discovery Tool deployment across critical infrastructure
National Cryptography Center | UAE cryptography body whose requirements the national Crypto Discovery Tool is customised to
DESC | Dubai Electronic Security Center, issued a Post-Quantum Cryptography Guideline
mainRegulation:
National Encryption Policy and its executive regulation | national | binding-law
National Post-Quantum Migration Programme and national Crypto Discovery Tool | national | guidance
DESC Post-Quantum Cryptography Guideline (Dubai) | national | guidance
obligation: Government entities must hold formally approved plans to migrate from traditional encryption (RSA, ECC and similar) to post-quantum cryptography under the National Encryption Policy and its executive regulation, approved in November 2025, and are expected to maintain an inventory of cryptographic assets and build crypto-agility into new systems. The mandate is a migration obligation overseen by the Cyber Security Council, not the adoption of a national algorithm. The Dubai Electronic Security Center guideline applies within the emirate of Dubai and is advisory.
classificationBasis:
summary + govActors (Cyber Security Council): National Encryption Policy mandating approved transition plans from traditional to post-quantum cryptography, backed by an executive regulation, and the National Post-Quantum Migration Programme and Crypto Discovery Tool announcements (included results rows, including WAM)
dominantProcess Sovereign: the UAE runs its own national programme - a National Encryption Policy, a National Cryptography Center setting requirements for the Crypto Discovery Tool, and a National Post-Quantum Migration Programme. No NIST FIPS suite is adopted as the named national standard in the included summaries; TII's submissions to NIST are research participation, not national adoption - REVIEW
govActors (ATRC): ATRC partnership announcements on the national post-quantum security transition and QuantumGate Crypto Discovery Tool deployment across critical national infrastructure (included results rows)
govActors (DESC): DESC Post-Quantum Cryptography Guideline launched at GISEC Global 2025 (included results row)
hybridDeployment None stated: no included document states a hybrid position
standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED. TII contributed MiRitH, PERK and RYDE to the NIST additional-signatures call and released the first UAE PQC library, but these are research-capacity items, not a national adopted standard or algorithm set
migrationTimeline / targetCompletion: OMITTED, no dated milestones or completion date stated in the included documents
